name: More Than You Ever Wanted to Know About Hash
recipeInstructions:
1. Start with raw potatoes for the best hash browns.
2. Peel and prepare the spuds as desired (diced for hash browns, sliced thin for home fries, grated for latkes).
3. For diner-style potatoes, parboil them the night before by dropping into rapidly boiling water, returning to a boil, and stirring occasionally until done but not mushy.
4. Drain completely and rinse under cold running water until no longer warm; this prevents overcooking in the skillet.
5. Store cooled potatoes in a sealed container in the refrigerator overnight.
6. The next morning, heat a skillet or griddle to where a drop of water dances on the surface.
7. Add enough butter/margarine/oil to lightly coat all the spuds.
8. Place cold potatoes into hot oil and fry constantly, turning until evenly browned and crispy on both sides.
9. Use a spatula to break them loose from the pan and transfer to a serving plate.
10. Enjoy your perfectly fried hash browns!
